questions.question_text: What does the pretraining stage produce?

options.text: A search engine that retrieves facts
A document completer that predicts what comes next
A model with no knowledge at all
A helpful assistant that follows instructions

questions.question_text: Which of these is a "fingerprint" left by fine-tuning?

options.text: The context window size
Token prediction
The knowledge cutoff
Sycophancy — agreeing too readily under pushback

questions.question_text: When generative AI writes an answer, what is it fundamentally doing?

options.text: Searching a database for the best match
Running calculations to derive the answer
Consulting a list of verified facts
Predicting what text comes next, one fragment at a time

questions.question_text: Where does hallucination tend to concentrate?

options.text: In specific details like names, dates, citations, and URLs
In general explanations of common concepts
In the opening sentence of a response
In formatting and structure
The model's training data has a fixed end date — it knows nothing after that unless it connects to another source
The model can only handle a limited number of topics
The model forgets information over long conversations
The model refuses to answer certain topics

questions.question_text: How is Working Memory different from the other three properties?

options.text: It improves automatically over time
It has a cliff rather than a gradual degradation — things work until they suddenly don't
It only matters for coding tasks
It can't be extended by product features
The model prioritizing length over quality
The model following an instruction literally but missing what you actually meant
The model generating text that's too formal
The model refusing to follow any instructions

questions.question_text: A hallucinated citation (a paper that doesn't exist) is which two properties colliding?

options.text: Working Memory + Steerability
Knowledge + Steerability
Next Token Prediction + Knowledge
Next Token Prediction + Working Memory

questions.question_text: What does "calibrated trust" mean in practice?

options.text: Trusting AI more as you use it more
Never using AI for important tasks
Always verifying every AI output
Locating your task on each property's continuum and adjusting your habits accordingly

questions.question_text: How do the four properties relate to the 4D Framework?

options.text: The 4Ds are what you do; the properties are what you're responding to when you do them
They're unrelated — two separate systems
The properties only apply to Discernment
They replace the 4Ds entirely

questions.question_text: How confident do you feel in your ability to apply the AI Capabilities and Limitations Framework concepts to your daily work?

options.text: Extremely unconfident
Not very confident
Neutral
Somewhat confident
Very confident

questions.question_text: How likely are you to recommend this course to a friend or colleague?

options.text: Not likely
Neutral
Very unlikely
Somewhat likely
Very likely

questions.question_text: Please share any feedback to help us improve this or future courses
